name: Sanctuary of Lord via Tossal de Vallonga Trail
name_original: Santuario de Lord — Valle de Lord
description: Hike the moderate 5.1-mile Sanctuary of Lord via Tossal de Vallonga Trail in Vall de Lord, featuring reservoir views and the historic Sanctu
region_intro: Hiking trails around Sant Llorenç De Morunys are situated in Catalonia, at the base of the Port del Comte mountain range within the Pyrenees. The region features diverse landscapes, including the Pantà de la Llosa del Cavall reservoir and river valleys like the Cardener. Geological formations of conglomerate material have created unique crags and monoliths throughout the area. This terrain offers varied hiking experiences, from river walks to mountain ascents. Best hiking trails around Sant Llorenç De Morunys The most popular hiking route is Sanctuary of Lord via Tossal de Vallonga Trail, a 5.2 miles (8.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route ascends to the Sanctuary of Lord, offering views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sources of Cardener — Valley of Lord, a moderate 10.1 miles (16.3 km) path. This trail follows the Cardener river through natural surroundings. Local hikers also love the Font de la Puda Sulphur Spring – La Coma Waterfall loop from Sant Llorenç de Morunys, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail leading through river valleys and past waterfalls, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Sant Llorenç De Morunys is defined by Pyrenean mountain terrain, river valleys, and unique conglomerate rock formations. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Sant Llorenç De Morunys are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 600 hikers have used komoot to explore Sant Llorenç De Morunys's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Sant Llorenç De Morunys?
A: There are over 25 hiking routes in the Sant Llorenç De Morunys area, offering a variety of experiences from moderate walks to more challenging ascents.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Sant Llorenç De Morunys?
A: The region is characterized by its Pyrenean mountain terrain, unique conglomerate rock formations, and river valleys. You can expect to see the picturesque &lt;strong&gt;Pantà de la Llosa del Cavall&lt;/strong&gt; reservoir, the Cardener river, and distinctive crags and monoliths formed by the local geology. Higher up, the &lt;strong&gt;Serra de Busa&lt;/strong&gt; offers incredible panoramic views.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, several trails lead to notable landmarks. You can hike to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927613294/sanctuary-of-lord-via-tossal-de-vallonga-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Lord&lt;/a&gt;, a historic site situated over a thousand meters high. Another popular destination is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927588110/sources-of-cardener-valley-of-lord" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sources of the Cardener river&lt;/a&gt;. For a unique natural monument, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4498468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capolatell (Busa Prison)&lt;/a&gt; on the Serra de Busa.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Sant Llorenç De Morunys?
A: The diverse landscapes of Sant Llorenç De Morunys are enjoyable throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer is ideal for higher altitude trails in the Port del Comte area. Some trails may be accessible in winter, but conditions can vary, especially at higher elevations.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Sant Llorenç De Morunys?
A: Yes, the area offers routes suitable for families. While most trails are moderate, there is at least one easy route available. Look for trails that follow river valleys or have less elevation gain for a more relaxed family outing.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Sant Llorenç De Morunys?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in the Sant Llorenç De Morunys region. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or protected wildlife, and to check for any specific local regulations or signage at trailheads.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. For example, you can enjoy the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14951971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de la Puda Sulphur Spring – La Coma Waterfall loop from Sant Llorenç de Morunys&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through river valleys and past a waterfall. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14951962" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sant Llorenç de Morunys – Sanctuary of Lord loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Sant Llorenç De Morunys?
A: Sant Llorenç De Morunys itself serves as a good starting point for many trails, and parking is generally available within the village. For specific trailheads outside the village, look for designated parking areas, which are often indicated on detailed route maps or descriptions.

Q: What is the difficulty level of hiking trails in Sant Llorenç De Morunys?
A: The trails cater to a range of abilities. While the majority of the 25 routes are classified as moderate, there is at least one easy option and one difficult trail for those seeking a greater challenge. This variety ensures options for different fitness levels and hiking experiences.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on hikes in Sant Llorenç De Morunys?
A: Yes, you can discover waterfalls in the region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14951971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font de la Puda Sulphur Spring – La Coma Waterfall loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically leads you past the La Coma Waterfall, offering a refreshing natural sight.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Sant Llorenç De Morunys?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the unique geological formations, and the rewarding views from elevated points like the Serra de Busa and the Sanctuary of Lord.

Q: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation in Sant Llorenç De Morunys?
A: Yes, Sant Llorenç De Morunys is a charming village with historical character. It offers amenities such as cafes and accommodation, making it a convenient base for exploring the surrounding hiking trails and enjoying the local culture.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sanctuary of Lord via Tossal de Vallonga Trail?
faqs.answer: Parking is generally available near the Sanctuary of Lord, which serves as a common starting point for many routes in the area. Specific parking details for the Tossal de Vallonga section may vary, but look for designated areas around the Sanctuary.

faqs.question: Is the Sanctuary of Lord via Tossal de Vallonga Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas like the Serres de Busa-els Bastets-Lord, and to ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike this trail is typically during spring and autumn when the weather is milder. Summer can be quite hot, especially in the exposed sections, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potential snow at higher elevations in the Pre-Pyrenees.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in the Vall de Lord area?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking the trails around the Sanctuary of Lord or in the broader Vall de Lord area. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied terrain, typical of the Pre-Pyrenees. You can expect mostly accessible paths, but some sections may be rugged or steep, especially as you ascend towards Tossal de Vallonga. Good hiking shoes are recommended for sure-footedness.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several interesting points. You can visit the beautiful Sanctuary of Lord itself, and enjoy stunning vistas from the Josep Cases Viewpoint (Pantà de la Llosa del Cavall), which offers magnificent views over the reservoir. You'll also pass by the Canal of Canalda.

faqs.question: Is this a circular trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: This specific route, the Sanctuary of Lord via Tossal de Vallonga Trail, is designed as a circular hike. It starts and ends at the same point, allowing you to experience diverse scenery without retracing your steps entirely.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'moderate' difficulty rating, this trail requires good fitness and sure-footedness. While not overly technical, it involves a significant distance and elevation gain. Beginners with some hiking experience and good physical condition should be able to complete it, but it's not ideal for absolute novices.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other trails in the area. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Santuari de Lord pel Tossal de Vallonga, Sallort, Sant Serni del Grau-Santuari del Lord, El Santuari, E05, La Bòfia, Sender Històric: Catalunya, Camí de Sant Jaume, and Camí ral de Solsona.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Yes, near the Sanctuary of Lord and in the nearby town of Sant Llorenç de Morunys, you can find various facilities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. It's a good idea to check opening hours, especially outside of peak season.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this trail?
faqs.answer: For a moderate hike like this, you should bring sturdy hiking shoes, sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and appropriate layers of clothing for changing weather conditions. A map or GPS device is also recommended.

waypoints.name: Josep Cases Viewpoint (Pantà de la Llosa del Cavall)
